_comment: Oakland encampment property destruction case — edit fields for each client

claim.reason: On approximately June 21, 2024, the City of Oakland Department of Public Works conducted an encampment sweep at 7th St & Castro St, Oakland, CA and seized and destroyed Plaintiff's personal property without adequate notice, without providing an opportunity to retrieve belongings, and without following the City's bag-and-tag policy. Plaintiff's property included clothing, sleeping equipment, tools, and personal documents. The City's actions violated the Fourth Amendment, Article I § 13 of the California Constitution, and the City's own policies.
claim.damages_calculation: Property destroyed by City of Oakland DPW: clothing (~$500), sleeping gear (~$300), tools (~$400), documents and personal items (~$300). Total property loss: ~$1,500. Non-economic damages for emotional distress, trauma, and setback to housing stability: ~$8,500. Total: $10,000.

declaration.content: I am the plaintiff in this action. On approximately June 21, 2024, the City of Oakland Department of Public Works conducted an encampment sweep at 7th St & Castro St, Oakland, CA. Without adequate notice and without providing an opportunity to retrieve my belongings, City employees seized and destroyed my personal property. My property included clothing, sleeping equipment, tools, and personal documents. At no time did City employees tag or store my property for later retrieval as required by the City's own bag-and-tag policy. I have been unable to replace most of these items and have suffered ongoing hardship as a result. I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is true and correct.
